hero.name: Mohamed Dyae Chellaf
hero.title: Full-Stack Developer
hero.subtitle: Software Engineering student at ENSEIRB-MATMECA, passionate about building modern, scalable, and elegant web applications.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Get to know me
about.p1: I'm a passionate Full-Stack Developer currently in my final year of Computer Science Engineering, specializing in Software Engineering at ENSEIRB-MATMECA Bordeaux-INP. My journey has allowed me to develop strong expertise in designing and building modern web applications.
about.p2: With experiences in France and internationally (Netherlands), I have strong skills in microservices architectures, front-end and back-end development, as well as DevOps and Cloud practices. I strive to combine technical rigor and creativity to deliver elegant and high-performance solutions.
about.p3: Passionate about technological innovation, I'm always looking for new challenges that push my boundaries and allow me to contribute to projects with real impact.
about.educationSubtitle: Education is now grouped here to keep the page more compact.

values.title: My Values
values.qualityDesc: Clean code, testing, and best practices

atos.desc1: Contributes to the redesign of the Orion information system, a modular and scalable ERP built on a microservices architecture, supporting the current and future needs of the Oui Care Group.
atos.desc2: Participates in the technical and functional design of a platform integrating CRM, HR, and SAP-based operational systems for personal services activities.
atos.desc3: Develops backend and frontend features using Spring Boot and Angular, with a strong focus on unit testing, code quality, and maintainability.
atos.desc4: Takes part in deployment and service industrialization within a cloud-based AWS environment.
atos.desc5: Works in an Agile development environment, actively participating in sprint ceremonies and collaboration with cross-functional teams.
atos.desc6: Contributes to business-critical modules related to billing, payroll, and accounting across the group's different brands.

labri.desc1: Developed a cartographic web interface to visualize over 25,000 collision alerts in real-time (Next.js, React, Leaflet, FastAPI).
labri.desc2: Designed, labeled, and trained a collision risk detection module using Machine Learning (Random Forest, Python, scikit-learn).
labri.desc3: Integrated real-time data pipelines for processing and displaying geolocated alerts.

vtec.desc4: Built a robust REST API using Node.js and Express for efficient data management and secure communication.
vtec.desc5: Integrated AWS DynamoDB (NoSQL) for storage and implemented user authentication with AWS Cognito.
vtec.desc6: Managed data flow through AWS SDK and API Gateway, ensuring scalability and reliability.
vtec.desc7: Collaborated within an international English-speaking team, participating in weekly scrums and code reviews.
vtec.desc8: Applied Agile methodology with continuous integration, iterative development, and cross-functional collaboration.
vtec.desc9: Contributed to technical documentation and continuous improvement of the product lifecycle.

rabat.desc1: Gained hands-on experience with the company's technical environment and internal management tools.
rabat.desc2: Analyzed the local network and backend server architecture to improve data centralization and file sharing.
rabat.desc3: Contributed to updating internal databases, digitizing administrative documents, and modernizing internal processes.

traffic.title: Campus Traffic Observatory
traffic.description: Client-side cartographic application built with SolidJS, Vite, and MapLibre GL for real-time visualization of campus mobility data: traffic, public transport, parking, bikes, weather, and air quality.

features: Interactive 2D/3D map powered by MapLibre GL JS with terrain elevation and WebGL rendering
Real-time public transport tracking (Trams & Buses) via GTFS-Realtime Protobuf decoding
Traffic analysis with Z-score normalization and color-coded road segments
Animated traffic simulation with cars moving along GeoJSON LineStrings using Turf.js
Weather widget with AQI calculation following US-EPA standards (PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3)
Multimodal fuzzy search using Fuse.js in Web Workers with Nominatim geocoding fallback
GTFS data pipeline compressing 160 Mo+ of transit data into optimized JSON at build time
Parking and bike-sharing (VCUB) station availability from Bordeaux Métropole open data
4-level test strategy: unit (Vitest), integration, component, and E2E (Playwright)
CI/CD with GitHub Actions, Vercel edge deployment, and proxy rewrites for CORS handling
details.architecture: Client-side SPA built with SolidJS and Vite for fine-grained reactivity without a virtual DOM. MapLibre GL JS handles WebGL-based 2D/3D cartographic rendering. Data sourced from CEREMA, TBM GTFS, open-meteo, Bordeaux Métropole, and Overpass/OSM APIs, pre-processed at build time. Real-time polling via Vite proxy (30s GTFS-RT, 5m parking). Search runs off-thread in Web Workers with Fuse.js. Deployed on Vercel edge CDN.

campus.title: Campus Mobility Analysis
campus.description: React cartographic interface for campus mobility data analysis and visualization, with video processing and metrics extraction via Python/YOLO.

features: Interactive Leaflet map with OpenStreetMap tiles for campus sensor visualization
Dual interface with Receiver mode for imports and sensor management, and Simulator mode for analytics
Vehicle detection from videos using YOLOv11 combined with ByteTrack tracking
Custom fine-tuning to add scooter detection to the base model
Statistical filtering using z-score normalization to remove anomalies and duplicates
D3.js charts for temporal series and vehicle-type distribution
Heatmap visualization with quantile-based traffic density coloring
FastAPI backend handling sensor CRUD, CSV uploads, and aggregated statistics
Multi-threaded video processing for long recordings
Agile project organization with weekly sprints and client reviews
details.architecture: Client-server architecture with a Next.js frontend built on React and TypeScript, Leaflet for mapping, and D3.js for data visualization. The backend is a Python FastAPI REST API connected to PostgreSQL through SQLAlchemy. Video analysis relies on YOLOv11 and ByteTrack, and the platform is organized around Receiver and Simulator workflows.

techStack.name: Next.js
techStack.role: Frontend framework with routing and rendering optimizations

transport.title: Holitrip - Travel Planning App
transport.description: Modular Java travel-planning application combining transport routing (BFS), hotels, and activities. Built with clean architecture, 124 tests, 93% coverage (JaCoCo), and 77% mutation score (PIT).

features: Transport search with BFS-based multi-leg routing and connection constraints
Hotel search by city, minimum rating, and budget limits
Activity search with category filtering and Haversine-distance proximity checks
Automatic package building combining transport, lodging, and activities
Geocoding integration for address-to-coordinate conversion
107 isolated unit tests using mocks and dependency injection
17 integration tests validating service interactions end to end
JaCoCo coverage reaching 93% on instructions and 91% on lines
PIT mutation analysis with a 77% kill rate
Service-based architecture designed for maintainability and testability
TDD methodology: tests written before implementation to drive design decisions
details.architecture: Modular Java 17 application built with Maven and organized around service interfaces plus dedicated implementations. The core domain combines transport routing, hotel search, activity search, geocoding, distance calculation, and package assembly. The design favors dependency injection and test doubles, while JSON files provide the project data sources.

techStack.name: Java
techStack.role: Core language for the business logic and clean architecture

collision.title: AI Collision Detection
collision.description: Interactive road-safety platform visualizing 25,000+ CAM/DENM collision alerts on a Leaflet map, with a FastAPI backend and a scikit-learn random-forest prediction pipeline.

features: Interactive Leaflet map for vehicles, buses, cyclists, and DENM events
Filtering by alert type and station ID with dynamic legend updates
Visualization of more than 25,000 alerts extracted from CAM and DENM messages
FastAPI backend serving partitioned JSON datasets for smooth browsing
Collision-risk prediction based on CAM-derived features
Automatic generation of proximity pairs using spatial and temporal thresholds
Rule-based auto-labeling with speed, heading, and distance heuristics
RandomForestClassifier training pipeline with exported joblib model
Prediction script producing `predicted_collision` outputs for new samples
Evaluation with classification report, confusion matrix, and precision above 0.94
details.architecture: Two-part platform combining a web application and a machine-learning pipeline. The web layer uses Next.js, React Leaflet, and CSS Modules, while the backend relies on FastAPI and locally stored JSON datasets. In parallel, a Python pipeline preprocesses trajectories, generates candidate pairs, labels collisions, trains a scikit-learn Random Forest model, and applies predictions to new situations.

techStack.role: Interactive alert visualization on the map

techStack.role: Data processing, scripting, and backend logic

realEstate.title: Real Estate Listings Manager
realEstate.description: Full-stack CRUD application for property listings, built with React + Vite and an Express + TypeScript backend validated with Zod.

features: Full CRUD flow for property listings
Layered Express API with routes, services, schemas, and middleware
Centralized input validation through reusable Zod middleware
React + Vite frontend with listing, detail, and form pages
Modern glassmorphism-inspired UI with reusable cards and modal dialogs
JSON persistence in `backend/data/properties.json`
details.architecture: Full-stack TypeScript application with an Express backend and a React + Vite frontend. The backend separates routes, services, models, schemas, and validation middleware, while the frontend is organized around pages, reusable components, API services, and shared types.

techStack.name: TypeScript
techStack.role: Strong typing on both frontend and backend

techStack.role: Frontend tooling and fast development server

budget.title: Selego Budget Tracker
budget.description: Budget management platform for projects and expenses with dashboards, notifications, email alerts, and AI-assisted expense categorization.

features: Project and expense management with budget tracking
Dashboard with statistics and budget-overrun visibility
Notification center with automatic email alerts
AI-assisted expense categorization
History, settings, search, and help center pages
JWT-based authentication with protected API access
details.architecture: Two-part JavaScript platform with a React frontend and a Node.js plus Express backend backed by MongoDB. The backend isolates third-party services such as Brevo, Passport, AI categorization, and S3 uploads, while the frontend centralizes API access and client state around reusable workflows.

threads.title: User-Space Threads Library
threads.description: C implementation of a user-space threading library with optional preemption, priority handling, tests, Valgrind checks, and performance graphs.

features: User-space thread library running on top of a single kernel thread
Optional preemption and thread priorities enabled through Makefile flags
Dedicated test suite with pthread comparison targets
Valgrind execution for memory validation
Performance graph generation through Python tooling
Install target producing binaries and a shared library
details.architecture: Low-level systems project centered on a C implementation of the threading runtime, supported by a test suite, installation outputs, and a Python tool for performance visualization. Build-time macros switch between feature variants such as preemption, priorities, and pthread comparison.

techStack.name: C
techStack.role: Systems-level implementation of the threading library
